creator: empty.japan
caption:
This experience could easily have been a 10 out of 10. The house is absolutely stunning with one half being stylishly renovated, while the other half retains much of its original charm, making it one of the most unique and best stays I’ve ever had in Japan—especially considering its relative affordability. Additionally, it’s located in a small, charming village, surrounded by beautiful nature. 

However, it does get quite cold inside, and the air conditioners simply couldn’t handle the colder weather, often shutting down to take a break. This isn’t much of an issue in summer, but it will stop me from visiting during colder months. Last but not least, the kitchen faucet was almost unusable due to low water pressure. 

Despite these issues, it was one of the best guest houses I’ve ever visited! My thanks to @villa_kuon_kisa for a wonderful stay! Check their page for location and other details ❤️
 *NOT sponsored!
